steps.title: Identify what the second sentence does
steps.content: The first sentence makes a general claim: sleek vehicles are more aerodynamic than bulky ones. The second sentence immediately introduces a specific aircraft (F-4 Phantom) with a specific design feature. This shift from general principle to concrete example signals what kind of transition is needed.

steps.title: Select the transition that introduces examples
steps.content:
For example transitions from a general statement to a specific instance illustrating it. Here, the F-4 Phantom's streamlined nose becomes the concrete case demonstrating why sleek vehicles are more aerodynamic. However signals contradiction, meanwhile implies simultaneous events, and additionally marks a separate supporting point—none match a transition from general principle to specific example.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
